Good news, Walyson. I spoke to my director, and he said the deal was approved. So we have an agreement.
That is great. So as agreed, the price is three thirty-five with free freight. What are the next steps?
The first deliverable is the signed order form. It is the only outstanding item, and I need it by Friday.
I will send the form by Friday so we can proceed. After that, can we finalize the shipping timeline?
Yes. Once the form is in, we finalize the schedule and move forward. I will send a written summary today.
Perfect. I am fully on board. I will confirm in writing, and then we both sign off.
As agreed, the price is three thirty-five with free freight.

Hi Walyson, this is Clara from the trading desk. Now that we have agreed on the price, I want to confirm the deal in writing and tell you the next steps. Here is a quick voice note before my email. As agreed, the price is three dollars thirty-five per kilo, with free freight, for twenty containers. I told my director that we had reached an agreement, and he said the deal was approved, so we can proceed. In my email I will confirm the figure, list the outstanding items, and give you the timeline. The first deliverable is the signed order form, which I need by Friday. After that, I said we would finalize the shipping schedule and move forward. Please reply to confirm you are happy, and we will both sign off. Thank you for a fair negotiation.

This is my recap note before I write the confirmation email to Clara. My goal is to confirm the final agreement clearly and list the next steps in writing, so nothing is lost. First, I will summarize what we agreed: I will say that the price is three dollars thirty-five with free freight for twenty containers, as agreed. Then I will report what Clara told me. She said the deal was approved by her director, and she told me the order form was the first deliverable. I will confirm that I can proceed and that the form is outstanding on my side until Friday. After that, I said we would finalize the shipping timeline and move forward together. I will keep the tone professional, repeat the key figures so there is no doubt, and ask her to confirm before we both sign off.

First, open the email and thank the other side for the agreement. Next, confirm the final terms clearly, for example the price, the volume, and the freight, using as agreed. After that, report what was decided with reported speech, for example she said the deal was approved. Then, list the next steps and the outstanding items, such as the signed order form and the shipping timeline. Finally, give a clear deadline, ask the other side to confirm, and say that you will both sign off and move forward.
